concept: A study of a dying garden where pressed, dried flowers are preserved behind layers of cracked, aged varnish and oxidized copper plating.
narrative: The weapon serves as a relic from a forgotten conservatory. As the floral patterns transition from the stock to the barrel, they shift from vibrant, saturated pigments to desiccated, charcoal-toned skeletons, reflecting the passage of time and the fragility of nature against industrial steel.

elements.generation_description: A large, semi-wilted peony flower with curling, translucent petal edges, rendered in fine-line ink illustration.
A macro texture of fine, spiderweb-like cracks in old lacquer, creating a sense of aged material depth.
Intertwining, thin, dry thorny vines that bridge the negative space between larger floral motifs.
Single, wind-blown, curling petals resting on the barrel area, showing signs of browning edges.
Subtle, tarnished brass ornamental flourishes that outline the receiver edges.
Brittle, translucent leaves showing prominent vein structures, layered behind flowers.
Micro-specks of light-colored debris scattered unevenly across the dark surfaces to imply a forgotten room.
Soft, monochromatic ink bleeding patterns that transition between the botanical elements.

pattern_notes: The design utilizes a heavy 'aged lacquer' overlay that creates a high-gloss finish over the matte floral ink work.
Floral density should be highest on the receiver and decrease as the pattern moves toward the muzzle.
Avoid solid geometric repeats; favor an organic, asymmetrical arrangement that mimics an overgrown garden wall.

stock.narrative_role: Introduction to the garden, featuring the most intact and saturated peony blooms.

receiver.narrative_role: The heart of the composition, where hero flowers meet encroaching ink smudges and brass framing.

magazine.narrative_role: Dense cluster of thorny vines and skeletonized leaves wrapping around the curvature.

pistol_grip.narrative_role: A singular, decaying leaf motif serving as a transition point.

handguard.narrative_role: Vines extending forward, thinning out as they move toward the front assembly.

front_assembly.narrative_role: The end of the growth, showing only subtle, faded vine wisps.

barrel_muzzle.narrative_role: Quiet, muted transition to oxidized metal, with only light dust motes remaining.

evaluation_criteria: Floral elements must feel organic and hand-illustrated rather than procedurally generated.
The contrast between the 'fresh' botanical colors and the 'aged' varnish must be legible at a distance.
The density of the pattern must successfully decline from stock to barrel to avoid visual noise at the muzzle.
reference_policy: Strictly adheres to historical botanical illustration styles and classical ink wash techniques; no references to living digital artists.
